title: podman build fails "bud and test inherit-labels" test
body:
### Issue Description

When first vendoring in Buidlah v1.40.0, the Buildah bud test "bud and test inherit-labels" fails with the below error:

```
   # podman build --force-rm=false --layers=false --signature-policy /root/tsweeney/workspaces/containers/podman/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.40.0/tests/policy.json --inherit-labels=false --label name=world -t exp -f /root/tsweeney/workspaces/containers/podman/src/github.com/containers/podman/test-buildah-v1.40.0/tests/bud/base-with-labels/Containerfile
   STEP 1/3: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal
   STEP 2/3: RUN echo hello
   hello
   STEP 3/3: LABEL "name"="world"
   COMMIT exp
   Getting image source signatures
   Copying blob sha256:ccf94125970dd961c73fa55a18348dabbd04cfaa6e475d98e86e9033577c5c53
   Copying blob sha256:39cc738e6e8324b282ba5de774b31b8956e9242f08efd1b7d1b3bddbee49bf03
   Copying config sha256:397d72416057f137e31225c2461a63d7ae0efd0b8369908b1a8dee5646bb8f03
   Writing manifest to image destination
   --> 397d72416057
   Successfully tagged localhost/exp:latest
   397d72416057f137e31225c2461a63d7ae0efd0b8369908b1a8dee5646bb8f03
   # buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} exp
   map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "license":"MIT" "name":"world" "org.opencontainers.image.license":"MIT" "org.opencontainers.image.name":"fedora-minimal" "org.opencontainers.image.url":"https://fedoraproject.org/" "org.opencontainers.image.vendor":"Fedora Project" "org.opencontainers.image.version":"42" "vendor":"Fedora Project" "version":"42"]
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} exp
   #| expected: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "name":"world"]'
   #|   actual: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.40.0" "license":"MIT" "name":"world" "org.opencontainers.image.license":"MIT" "org.opencontainers.image.name":"fedora-minimal" "org.opencontainers.image.url":"https://fedoraproject.org/" "org.opencontainers.image.vendor":"Fedora Project" "org.opencontainers.image.version":"42" "vendor":"Fedora Project" "version":"42"]'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

This seems to be working Buildah's tests, I'm not sure what the difference is in Podman's test suite.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Seen when vendoring Buildah v1.40, or by running ./test/buildah-bud/run-buildah-bud-tests
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

See description.  This test has been turned off in Podman.

### Describe the results you expected

Happy tests when vendoring.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: This seems like a real bug - we're inheriting labels we shouldn't be inheriting?
Could the `--inherit-labels` flag to `podman build` be broken?
Like with any new build option it must be wired properly into the podman cli/API layers.
